tool_input.summary: To assess whether Jezero carbonate detections are spatially associated with the short-wavelength (“blue”) end of the olivine 1 µm band range versus a longer-wavelength (“redshifted”) olivine unit, the most relevant PDS archives are CRISM map-projected cubes (MTRDR/MRDR) and CRISM spectral-parameter map products (ATO LMLP_PARAM), plus targeted hyperspectral RDRs for retrieving olivine band centers and carbonate bands from spectra.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Provides map-projected targeted CRISM reduced data (MTRDR) suitable for regional-scale compositional mapping around Jezero; you can derive olivine 1 µm band centers and carbonate absorptions consistently in map space.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MTRDR Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: The data collection within the CRISM MTRDR PDS4 archive where the map-projected targeted products live; useful for assembling a Jezero-region mosaic/stack for correlation of carbonate detections with olivine band position.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MPTARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 map-projected targeted RDRs (the PDS3 counterpart to the PDS4 MTRDR concept) commonly used for regional CRISM mineral mapping and comparison of carbonate detections with olivine spectral properties.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM MRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Map-projected reduced data record (MRDR) products that support broader-area context mapping (regional scale) to compare carbonate distributions with olivine spectral units beyond just individual targeted scenes.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-MULTISPECTRAL-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 multispectral RDRs for regional context; helpful for mapping broad olivine/carbonate patterns when hyperspectral coverage is sparse.

candidates.title: CRISM ATO S-Band LMLP_PARAM Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides map-projected CRISM spectral-parameter products (derived ‘parameter maps’) which can be used to delineate carbonate detections and olivine-related parameters regionally, then compare spatially against olivine band-center-derived units.

candidates.title: CRISM ATO S-Band LML_PARAM Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Sensor-space spectral-parameter products; useful if you want parameter mapping tied directly to the original observation geometry before map projection, then relate carbonate indicators to olivine spectral behavior.

candidates.title: CRISM ATO S-Band Log Maximum Likelihood Method Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Top-level bundle containing the CRISM ATO S-band maximum-likelihood-derived products and documentation; good entry point for understanding/using the parameter-map collections for carbonate/olivine regional comparisons.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-3-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Targeted CRISM RDRs (non-map-projected) appropriate for extracting/ fitting the olivine 1 µm band center and checking carbonate absorption features directly in spectra for Jezero-area targeted observations.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-4-RDR-TARGETED-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Additional targeted CRISM RDR holdings; complements the targeted dataset series for spectral analysis of olivine band position and carbonate features.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-5-RDR-VNIRHYPERSPECTRAL-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: VNIR hyperspectral RDRs that can be used to characterize the 1 µm olivine band shape/center (‘blue’ vs ‘redshifted’ olivine) in targeted scenes, then compare against carbonate detections.

candidates.title: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter CRISM VRDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: PDS4 bundle for CRISM VNIR reduced data (VRDR); a PDS4 route to VNIR hyperspectral products used for olivine 1 µm band characterization.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-4-TYPESPEC-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: CRISM Type Spectra library; useful as reference spectra for identifying carbonate signatures and interpreting olivine band-center shifts when analyzing Jezero-region CRISM data.

candidates.title: MRO-M-CRISM-2-EDR-V1.0
candidates.reasoning: Raw/less-processed CRISM EDRs if you need to apply custom calibration/atmospheric correction before deriving precise olivine band centers or carbonate detections.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
At a broad regional scale, are the carbonate detections in Jezero associated with the blue end of the olivine 1 micron band range or the redshifted olivine unit?
